agent.name: seo-creator
agent.concept_summary: An agent-driven engine that produces and governs SEO/GEO content hubs (a pillar page plus a funnel-staged cluster of long-form, E-E-A-T-grade guides) for agency clients in the senior-living / memory-care vertical. Lives at apps/seo in the Sagemark monorepo. A self-hosted Claude Agent SDK worker (on Vercel Sandbox) runs the autonomous generation loop; the deterministic non-compensatory quality gate, scorers, and lifecycle FSM (ported from flywheel-main origin/preview) are enforced host-side and the agent cannot reason past them.
agent.positioning: The model is the replaceable middle; the harness is the moat. A non-compensatory publish gate, a cross-model faithfulness check, a fail-closed lifecycle FSM, and per-tenant voice/corpus isolation produce a durable, crawlable, schema-marked content asset that compounds across human readers, search crawlers, and AI answer engines.

competitors.name: Jasper
competitors.description: AI marketing-content platform with per-brand voice + team approval flows
competitors.weakness: stops at generation; confidently states wrong facts; generic drafts; no gate/YMYL governance

competitors.name: Surfer SEO
competitors.description: Real-time content editor scoring drafts against ranking factors (0-100 Content Score)
competitors.weakness: compensatory score drives keyword-stuffing; users report rank drops; nudges, never refuses

competitors.name: Frase
competitors.description: Research briefs + AI writing + dual Google/AI scoring
competitors.scale: positioned as complete AI SEO agent
competitors.weakness: output requires editing; tier throttles; scores, doesn't gate

competitors.name: Byword
competitors.description: Bulk article generation for publishers, topic-set in / articles-to-CMS out
competitors.weakness: the anti-gate: makes publishing feel too easy; manual-action risk; no governance

competitors.name: Writesonic
competitors.description: AI content generation + publishing integrations at scale, pivoting to AI-search visibility

competitors.name: Search Atlas / OTTO
competitors.description: Agency all-in-one with OTTO agent that auto-deploys technical fixes via on-site pixel
competitors.weakness: pixel lock-in; auto-applies changes, no content refuse-gate

competitors.name: AirOps
competitors.description: Content-engineering platform for AI search; Brand Kits/Knowledge Bases + workflow automation
competitors.business_model: seat-based, $40M Series B at $225M valuation (Greylock, Nov 2025), $62.1M raised
competitors.scale: customers incl. Ramp, Webflow, Klaviyo, Kayak
competitors.differentiator: unified SEO+AI performance + per-tenant brand grounding
competitors.weakness: un-opinionated build-your-own pipeline; ships guidance/brand-voice consistency, NOT a refuse-to-publish gate — the strategic threat that could add a gate

competitors.name: Profound
competitors.description: Enterprise answer-engine analytics; brand mentions/citations/sentiment across 10+ AI engines

competitors.name: Goodie AI
competitors.description: GEO tool monitoring brand visibility/citations in AI responses + content optimization

competitors.name: AthenaHQ
competitors.description: GEO platform with citation-prediction metrics (ACE, QVEM) + brand-voice enforcement
competitors.differentiator: citation prediction; closest to governance language
competitors.weakness: voice-consistency only (not faithfulness/YMYL refusal); credit model unpredictable at scale

precedents.name: v0 (Vercel)
precedents.transferable_pattern: Agentic artifact pipeline whose reliability gains came from DETERMINISTIC autofixers + self-review before shipping the artifact — direct precedent for a fail-closed gate + faithfulness self-check; validates 'harness not model'
precedents.not_transferable: Code has a compiler (objective pass/fail); YMYL content has none — we must manufacture ground truth via cross-model faithfulness checks against sources

precedents.name: AirOps Brand Kits / Knowledge Bases
precedents.transferable_pattern: Per-tenant brand grounding for voice + proprietary data, matching our per-client voice-isolation shape
precedents.not_transferable: It's an un-opinionated toolkit; our value is the opinionated fail-closed FSM the agency does not assemble

precedents.name: GEO trackers (Profound/Goodie/AthenaHQ)
precedents.transferable_pattern: 'Share of model' / citation-frequency as the new success metric — instrument hubs for AI-answer-engine citation
precedents.not_transferable: Read-only measurement; they don't generate/govern the artifact, so complement not core

cautionary_tales.name: CNET 'Money Staff' AI finance articles (2023)
cautionary_tales.why_it_failed: Numeric errors ($10,300 vs correct $300), plagiarism, no pre-publish factual gate in a YMYL domain
cautionary_tales.lesson: A non-compensatory gate that blocks unverifiable numeric/financial claims is the control CNET lacked; the failure was process, not model

cautionary_tales.name: Sports Illustrated / AdVon (2023)
cautionary_tales.what_they_tried: Published reviews under fabricated AI-generated author personas
cautionary_tales.lesson: E-E-A-T must be provable: real bylines/credentials/sourcing as a checkable publish precondition; refuse to attach fabricated authority

cautionary_tales.name: AI content farms + Google AI Overviews health-misinfo (2024-26) + Babylon Health
cautionary_tales.what_they_tried: Scaled AI content / ungoverned AI health claims
cautionary_tales.why_it_failed: Mass deindexing post-Helpful-Content; Guardian 2026 probe found Google's own AI Overviews gave misleading health advice in 44% of medical searches; Babylon collapsed on over-claimed under-validated AI
cautionary_tales.lesson: For YMYL memory-care, a cross-model faithfulness check + YMYL refusal is the feature that survives the next core update and shields clients

user_sources.summary: The approved SEO Creator analysis docs + locked-decisions record (D1-D9) authored 2026-06-25 and git-verified against origin/preview
The reference output artifact: a senior-living SEO/GEO content hub (pillar + cluster of E-E-A-T guides)

steal_vs_avoid.pattern: deterministic autofixers + self-review before shipping artifact
steal_vs_avoid.why: direct precedent for fail-closed gate + faithfulness self-check; reliability came from the deterministic layer, validating harness-not-model

steal_vs_avoid.pattern: per-tenant brand grounding for voice/proprietary data
steal_vs_avoid.why: matches per-client voice-isolation shape; reuse grounding, then add the gate they lack
instrument hubs for AI-citation as the outcome metric; integrate downstream, not core
a compensatory green-score is the opposite of a non-compensatory refuse-gate; drives keyword-stuffing and rank drops

steal_vs_avoid.pattern: volume-first generate->CMS with no quality gate
steal_vs_avoid.why: makes publishing feel too easy — the exact failure mode our gate inverts; deindexing risk for YMYL clients

steal_vs_avoid.pattern: ungoverned YMYL generation + faked E-E-A-T authority
steal_vs_avoid.why: defines the harm to design against: enforce provable bylines/sourcing and refuse unverifiable claims as a publish precondition

steal_vs_avoid.source: Google AI Overviews health-misinfo (2026) / Babylon Health
steal_vs_avoid.pattern: ungoverned/over-claimed AI in health under regulator + press scrutiny
steal_vs_avoid.why: YMYL memory-care is actively burning; faithfulness check + YMYL refusal is the survival feature

steal_vs_avoid.source: A Place for Mom AIO pivot
steal_vs_avoid.pattern: largest senior-living aggregator moving keyword-SEO to truthful structured AIO content
steal_vs_avoid.why: validates beachhead demand + GEO direction in our exact vertical; watch as signal/channel/competitor

implications_for_discovery: Wedge confirmed: frame the PRD around governance (the gate) as the differentiator, not generation speed (table stakes).
AirOps ($40M Series B) is the displacement risk; defense is the opinionated fail-closed FSM + vertical E-E-A-T depth a horizontal toolkit won't prioritize.
GEO 'share of model' is now the success metric; instrument hubs for AI-answer-engine citation, not just rank — confirm as a product KPI.
The D2xD3 tension is market-validated: thin grounding is exactly what got content farms deindexed; instrument the gate-block-by-sourcing rate (the D3 reversal trigger).
E-E-A-T must be provable not claimed (SI/CNET): byline/credential/citation as hard publish preconditions; confirm credentialed-reviewer staffing (D6) as the binding go-live constraint.

questions.question: Wedge: why does the customer choose this over a generic LLM or ungoverned AI tool?
questions.answer: No shipping competitor can refuse to publish on faithfulness/YMYL grounds; the field is empty on governance (Phase 2 research across ~12 products).
questions.interpretation: Wedge is governance (the gate), not generation speed. Frame the PRD around refusal-as-feature.

questions.question: Moat: what can a competitor not replicate in 6 months?
questions.answer: The opinionated fail-closed lifecycle FSM + vertical senior-living E-E-A-T depth + per-client voice corpus.
questions.interpretation: Defensible vs a horizontal toolkit; AirOps ($40M) is the displacement risk and could add a gate, so vertical depth is the durable moat.

questions.question: Customer validation? (Trigger-3 commercial gate)
questions.answer: Whispering Willows is a real client engagement.
questions.interpretation: Strong validation; the demo reflects real client work. No abort.

questions.question: Who are the personas?
questions.answer: Operator (agency editor), client (reviews/signs off), credentialed reviewer (holds YMYL release authority).
questions.interpretation: Three distinct surfaces: operator console, client review preview, reviewer release gate.

questions.question: Capabilities (5-10)?
questions.answer: Autonomous brief->draft->gate->render loop; per-client voice; hub (pillar+cluster) + resource-library homepage generation; conversational fine-tune; on-screen client feedback; share-of-model instrumentation.

questions.question: Agent runtime?
questions.answer: Claude Agent SDK self-hosted worker on Vercel Sandbox (D5/D9); apps/seo on Vercel = thin UI + orchestration.
questions.interpretation: Split topology; worker runs the autonomous loop, Vercel relays SSE.

questions.question: Judge/gate placement?
questions.answer: Host-enforced non-compensatory gate + 22 scorers + cross-model faithfulness, ported from origin/preview; agent gets read-only scoring tools, cannot reason past a veto or canPublish().
questions.interpretation: The moat lives outside the loop; the agent can never self-grade past the gate.

questions.question: Multi-tenancy model?
questions.answer: Supabase, workspace_id + client_id, fail-closed RLS (anon SELECT only status=published).
questions.interpretation: Tenancy is a hard fail-closed boundary; voice bleed is the cardinal failure.

questions.question: Publish lifecycle / kill switch?
questions.answer: Fail-closed publish (verdict=PUBLISH AND recorded human release AND eval-ran; YMYL adds named author+credentials+citations). Disabling release halts public output without stopping drafting.
questions.interpretation: Publish transition is the chokepoint and the kill switch.

questions.question: State persistence given ephemeral Sandbox?
questions.answer: All per-run session/agent/working-dir state persists to Supabase (system of record), never the Sandbox filesystem.
questions.interpretation: Sandbox is compute-only; Supabase is durable truth.

questions.question: Top risk?
questions.answer: Cross-tenant leakage / voice bleed (the #1 agency-ending risk) + ungoverned YMYL content.
questions.interpretation: Fail-closed tenancy + no default-voice fallthrough + provable E-E-A-T preconditions.

questions.question: Ingestion surface?
questions.answer: Web-fetch brief route (SSRF / prompt-injection from fetched pages) — covered despite private-by-default.
questions.interpretation: SSRF-guarded fetch contract; treat fetched content as untrusted.

questions.question: D2xD3 grounding tension?
questions.answer: Hard gate + thin DDG grounding => high self-veto on YMYL drafts; instrument gate-block-by-sourcing rate as the D3 reversal trigger.
questions.interpretation: Market-validated by the content-farm deindexing tales; measured + reversible.

questions.question: Build constraints?
questions.answer: Existing Sagemark monorepo; ported engine collapses cost to ~4 producer prompts + worker topology + transport; Phase-1 scope inflated by D1+D2+D5+D7; solo founder + agents.
questions.interpretation: Sequence the thinnest end-to-end slice first before widening surface area.

questions.question: Pricing/packaging + wallet?
questions.answer: Per-seat / per-piece SaaS + separate AI-Gateway SEO ledger.
questions.interpretation: Productized SaaS; separate ledger measures cost-per-asset (incl. reviewer time) = the margin input.

questions.question: North-star KPI?
questions.interpretation: Instrument hubs for answer-engine citation; pair with a client-outcome metric later.

mid_domain_syntheses.playback: The wedge is governance, not speed; the moat is the fail-closed FSM + vertical depth.
The binding constraint at go-live is the credentialed reviewer (D6), not tokens or infra.
mid_domain_syntheses.human_confirmation: confirmed; logged as the top open-question

open_questions.question: Name the credentialed YMYL reviewer + a backup, and set the pages/week capacity ceiling.
Calibrate per-asset cost and finalize per-piece/seat pricing from the live SEO ledger after the first cluster.
Decide the D3 reversal: if gate-block-by-sourcing is the top blocker after the first cluster, fund a SERP/retrieval API.

synthesis_emphasis: Governance (the non-compensatory gate) is the differentiator — the PRD's governing thought.
Vertical senior-living E-E-A-T depth is the durable moat against horizontal incumbents (AirOps).
Share-of-model is the north-star KPI; instrument AI-answer-engine citation.
The credentialed reviewer (D6) is the binding go-live constraint — staffing is the top open-question.

stakeholders.specialist_qualification: health-content credential; holds release authority on YMYL content at publish time (product-level gate, not plan-level signoff)
stakeholders.contact: OPEN QUESTION — not yet named (top go-live blocker)

bootstrap_state.next_action: Hand to build-flywheel-blueprint (/seo-creator-build) or engineering team

assumptions.claim: No shipping competitor can refuse to publish on faithfulness/YMYL grounds; the field is empty on governance (the wedge).
assumptions.source: Phase 2 research across ~12 products (Jasper, Surfer SEO, Frase, Byword, Writesonic, Search Atlas, AirOps, Profound, Goodie, AthenaHQ); every tool nudges via a compensatory score or voice-consistency, none refuses to publish.
assumptions.validation_path: Re-run competitive scan by 2026-09-23 (commercial +90d trigger); monitor AirOps and AthenaHQ for refuse-to-publish gate-language drift.

assumptions.claim: Whispering Willows is a real paying client engagement and the demo reflects real client work (commercial validation).
assumptions.source: Signed client engagement (manifest stakeholders); live reference hub at whispering-willows-content-demo.vercel.app.
assumptions.validation_path: First paid pilot live + client sign-off on a generated cluster (success-section 60-day indicator).

assumptions.claim: Vertical senior-living E-E-A-T depth plus the opinionated fail-closed FSM is the durable moat that a horizontal incumbent (AirOps) will not prioritize within 6 months.
assumptions.source: Discovery Q1.2; AirOps ships an un-opinionated build-your-own toolkit, not a vertical gate (research-brief).
assumptions.validation_path: Track competitor roadmaps quarterly; measure vertical-depth lead (memory-care-specific gate rules + named clinician bylines) shipped vs. incumbent feature releases.

assumptions.claim: Share-of-model (AI-answer-engine citation, not rank) is the right north-star KPI.
assumptions.source: GEO trackers (Profound $35M Sequoia, Goodie, AthenaHQ) productize share-of-model; A Place for Mom Nov-2025 AIO pivot in the exact vertical; research-brief industry context.
assumptions.validation_path: Instrument hub citation frequency across at least 3 answer engines; confirm it moves with a client-outcome metric over the first 90 days.

assumptions.claim: Per-piece / per-seat SaaS pricing clears margin at the measured per-asset cost.
assumptions.source: Discovery Q6.2 default; the <=$2 editorial cost is a PRD target, not a measured number.
assumptions.validation_path: Measure cost-per-asset (incl. reviewer time) from the live AI-Gateway SEO ledger after the first Whispering Willows cluster; lock pricing only then (OQ-2).

assumptions.claim: One credentialed reviewer clears enough pages/week to make YMYL publish-rate viable.
assumptions.source: D6 unstaffed; no engineering default; the reviewer is the binding throughput constraint under the D2 hard gate.
assumptions.validation_path: Staff the named reviewer + backup (OQ-1), then measure actual pages/week cleared and approval-debt (open-thread count + cycle time) per client in Phase 3/4; set the capacity ceiling from observed throughput.

assumptions.claim: DuckDuckGo scraping (3 pages x 2,000 chars) grounds memory-care YMYL claims sufficiently to clear the hard faithfulness gate at acceptable throughput.
assumptions.source: D3 [OVERRIDE]; flagged D2xD3 tension in DECISIONS.md; content-farm deindexing cautionary tales show thin grounding is exactly what fails.
assumptions.validation_path: Instrument gate-block-by-sourcing rate (UNSOURCED_STAT + low-faithfulness from thin sources) in Phase 1; if sourcing is the top blocker after the first cluster, flip D3 to a funded SERP/retrieval API behind the same brief.sources contract (OQ-3).

assumptions.claim: The ported deterministic engine (seo-gate, lifecycle-fsm, 22 scorers, voice/faithfulness gates) behaves identically to the flywheel-main origin/preview source.
assumptions.source: Engine confirmed present at origin/preview (PRs #1668-1684); ported verbatim with production bug-fix scars preserved (faithfulness 12s timeout + 25-claim cap; voice 3s timeout; drafter !== verifier invariant).
assumptions.validation_path: Golden-set regression: capture the Whispering Willows hub as a human-labeled corpus (Phase 0); assert the ported gate reproduces expected Stage-A clean/veto + Stage-B verdict bands before any prompt is written, and on every kernel change.

review_log.verdict: Conditionally ready — strong plan; 5 must-fix gaps clustered on the split worker topology

review_log.reviewer_detail: Codex gpt-5.5 (xhigh) — cross-model adversarial reviewer, codex exec (round 1/4)
review_log.verdict: Strong governance-first plan; not production-ready as written — highest risk in operationalizing the Agent-SDK/Sandbox runtime control + durable web grounding

review_log.reviewer_detail: Codex gpt-5.5 (xhigh) — cross-model adversarial reviewer, codex exec (round 2/4)
review_log.verdict: Materially stronger than round 1; not yet production-ready — newly exposed contradictions (circular YMYL gate, BYOK ledger bypass, PRD/RFC name drift) block publish/accounting/ownership

review_log.reviewer_detail: Codex gpt-5.5 (xhigh) — cross-model adversarial reviewer, codex exec (round 3/4)
review_log.verdict: Converging — the gate/release split is coherent and the external-dependency ledger is complete; remaining blocker is that the release/signoff records are not yet in the data model

review_log.reviewer_detail: Codex gpt-5.5 (xhigh) — cross-model adversarial reviewer, codex exec (round 4/4, final)
review_log.verdict: Near-ready; the credentialed_release/client_signoff split landed durably. Two medium inconsistencies remained (thinnest-slice def, missing byline_authorizations table)

CONVERGED — internally consistent and ready to build; four-skill chain + kernel-route host-enforcement + release-split all coherent; 1 cosmetic documented rename (review_comments->comment_threads) accepted

trust_signal.qualitative_why: Research-grounded wedge, a 6-round cross-model gauntlet that converged at 0.93, git-verified reuse claims, and a clean lint. Held from 0.95 only by two honestly-ledgered unknowns (Sandbox capability-denial enforceability, DDG-grounding sufficiency under the hard YMYL gate), each with a de-risking spike/reversal-trigger.

retro.discovery_quality: Condensed (plan pre-existed); interviewed only the load-bearing gaps (validation/pricing/D6/KPI). Sharp.
retro.plan_quality: Strongest on gate/governance/tenancy + the corrected four-skill kernel-route architecture; thin spots caught and fixed by the gauntlet.
retro.research_brief_utility: High — confirmed the wedge, surfaced AirOps as the displacement risk, and the cautionary tales that ground the YMYL thesis.
retro.review_cycle_effectiveness: High — Claude and Codex caught complementary gap classes; trust 0.72->0.93 over 6 rounds (4 Codex, floor met).
retro.journeys_lint_signal: 12 journeys across all categories; lint caught a real NextSchool leak + schema completeness gaps.

improvement_suggestions.note: a prior-build product name inherited-bug reference leaked into PRD (3x) — generalized.
review_log missing completed_at; top-level handoff object not in schema — fixed.
lint #8-10 read PRs from a manifest.pr_map the schema does not define; should parse RFC ### PR NNN markdown.
HANDOFF template instructs writing a handoff manifest object the schema forbids (additionalProperties:false).
journey-measurability (#12) does not detect table-format metric thresholds; false-negative on well-formed journeys.

violations.message: H1 title (parse artifact), TL;DR (governing-thought prose), and Data model (decision TABLE) lack inline decision markers.
violations.accepted_with_reason: TL;DR is the Pyramid governing-thought summary; §10 is a decision table; the H1 title is a parser artifact. All carry decisions in adjacent/table form.
Dense markdown (127KB) with long prose lines; all 17 sections + 15a ledger + stakeholder map present; gauntlet-hardened to trust 0.93. The line-count heuristic undercounts dense tables/prose.
All 12 journeys carry >=5 measurable threshold rows as Markdown tables (verified); the lint metric-detector does not parse table-row thresholds. Filed as a blueprint improvement.
